title: IPHONE 17 PROMAX GÂY THẤT VỌNG CÙNG CỰC VÌ CHẤT LƯỢNG THUA CẢ ĐIỆN THOẠI TÀU:NHÀ TÁO HẾT THỜI RỒI Ư?
transcript: two As soon as Apple officially launched the iPhone 17 series, the atmosphere at Apple Stores from New York to Shanghai became more exciting than ever.  Long lines of people waited from early morning for a chance to own the phone that is considered the new standard in the smartphone world.  Not disappointing the expectations of the technology world, a report from market research firm CounterP Research shows that the standard version of the iPhone 17 is creating a real storm with sales 14% higher than the previous generation after only 10 days of opening for sale in two important markets, the US and China.  According to experts at Counterpoint Research, the success of the iPhone 17 comes from a combination of significant technical improvements and a reasonable pricing strategy.  The screen has higher brightness and smoother refresh rate.  The A19 chip with superior performance and larger storage capacity has helped this product line conquer users right from the first experiences. Consumers appreciate the standard iPhone 17 for its practical and useful improvements, said senior analyst Ivanam. In China, iPhone 17 sales almost doubled compared to iPhone 16 in the initial launch period and this trend continued until October. Analyst M Mang Meng Jiang also agreed, saying that Apple cleverly maintained the same price as iPhone 16 but brought more value to buyers.  The chip and screen are significantly improved, the storage memory is increased, the selfie camera is upgraded while the price remains unchanged.  With attractive discounts from retailers, the iPhone 17 becomes a bright choice in the high-end mid-range segment. Giang said that not only the standard iPhone 17 , the high-end version iPhone 17 Pro Max also recorded strong demand in the US.  Many major carriers like AT&T and Verizon have launched a series of financial support packages to help users easily upgrade their devices.  This policy not only helps Apple expand its loyal customer base but also pushes sales in the high-end mobile segment to record levels.  On the other hand, the iPhone e, a product aimed at ordinary users, although not as explosive as the two versions above, still has an encouraging start.  In China, the device even sold out within minutes of going on sale, showing that the appeal of the Apple brand has not diminished.  According to CMBC, thanks to strong sales of the iPhone 17 series, Apple shares rose to a record $262.24 in the most recent trading session.  To date, the iPhone business still accounts for nearly half of the total revenue of the world's largest technology corporation .  However, behind the sales boom are worrying reflections from the user community.  Last week, a Reddit account named DCECH 316 posted an image of his cosmic orange iPhone 17 Pro Max gradually turning to rose gold after only a few days of use.  This case quickly attracted attention when a series of other users spoke up to confirm the same situation .  According to Mac Roomer, there have been dozens of reports of the iPhone 17 Pro and iPhone 17 Pro Max in cosmic orange changing color after a short period of use. Some customers were given new devices by Apple after checking and confirming the error. Posts on TikTok and Reddit describe the phenomenon occurring primarily on the anodized aluminum frame surrounding the device.  While the rear glass remains the same, the aluminum frame and camera cluster gradually turn rose gold, so the color difference is easily noticeable.  Some people even reported that their devices changed color after just 4 days despite always being kept in the original clear case.   The initial cause is thought to be an oxidation reaction that occurs when the aluminum layer is exposed to a solution containing peroxide or ultraviolet light for a long time.  Apple says the aluminum frame of the iPhone 17 Pro and 17 Pro Max is coated with a special protective layer to limit oxidation. However, this coating may not have the desired effect.  If the device is exposed to strong cleaning chemicals or industrial alcohol , discoloration may occur more quickly.  Experts recommend that users should avoid using cleaning solutions containing high concentrations of alcohol or rough cloths when cleaning the device.  As this may cause the aluminum frame to become scratched or discolored.   The official instructions on the Apple website recommend gently wiping with 70% Isopropanol, 75% Ethylene Alcohol or using chlorine disinfectant wipes to clean the surface of the device.  So far, Apple has not given any official response regarding the color change issue on iPhone 17 Pro Max. However, some users claim to have had their devices exchanged after sending feedback to the warranty center.  This incident is causing the technology community to continue to discuss heatedly.  Because even though the iPhone 17 has achieved great success in sales, product quality still needs to be clearly explained by Apple to ensure user trust .  While Apple faces controversy, Chinese phone companies, once considered Chinese goods, are rising strongly, capturing market share and redefining the concept of cheap high-end phones.  In China, once Apple's largest market, the iPhone 17 recorded impressive sales in its first days of sale, but the increase did not last long.  The appearance of Huawei Mate 70, Xiaomi 15 and Honor Magic 7 series has quickly attracted domestic consumers .  According to a report by Counterpoint Research, in the third quarter of 2025, Huawei grew more than 37% in the Chinese market , surpassing Apple to take the top spot in the high-end phone segment.  This was almost unthinkable just three years ago when Huawei was heavily affected by US sanctions. Huawei's success does not come from a massive marketing strategy but from good control of domestic supply and strong investment in new generation Kirin chips.  This chip was developed by China itself, helping the company affirm its position of technological autonomy. In addition, the Mate 70's camera is rated superior to the iPhone 17 Pro Max in DC Max's tests. These improvements give consumers a clear reason to turn their backs on Apple rather than spend hundreds of dollars more on a device that's been controversial for its durability and shell quality.  Not only Huawei, other Chinese brands are also making significant progress.  Xiaomi is now expanding strongly into Europe and Southeast Asia with the Xiaomi 15 Ultra series, which is equipped with both LCK image sensor and configuration that surpasses the selling price.  In Vietnam, this model is continuously out of stock right after its launch according to statistics from Cellphoneet and Hoang Ha Mobile systems. Meanwhile, Oppo and Vivo continue to strengthen their position in the mid-range segment, where users look for a balance between performance and price.  OPPO Reno 12 and Vivo X100 are both integrated with the new generation Dity chip that supports AI to directly process images, providing a camera experience close to a professional camera.  These lines of phones have made consumers no longer see Chinese phones as cheap, low-quality products but as reasonable and reliable choices.  According to Canaus Q3 2025 report, the global Chinese phone share has surpassed 45% with Xiaomi, Oppo, Vivo and Huawei among the top six largest brands.  In contrast, Apple saw a slight decline in Asia and North America as users hesitated due to the increasing price and the limited number of actual improvements.  What is noteworthy is the change in consumer psychology.  A decade ago, the iPhone was seen as a status symbol. Today, that is no longer obvious. In big cities of China, Vietnam and Thailand, young users are willing to choose Xiaomi, Honor or Xiaomi phones not only because of price but because they feel that Chinese technology is now strong enough to be proud of.  Technology experts say Apple is facing its most difficult problem in many years, maintaining its brand value in a context where its competitors are no longer weak.  The strategy of polishing the brand through design and luxury is no longer enough to convince consumers.  Meanwhile, Chinese companies are gaining an advantage thanks to their fast pace of innovation, AI integration, superior cameras and flexible manufacturing capabilities.  A typical example is the Honor Magic 7, the world's first phone model to apply an independent image processing chip based on artificial intelligence to help take photos in low light conditions up to 80% clearer. The technology surprised many when it was discovered that it did not come from Sicol Vanley but from Shenzhen, which was once known only as a component manufacturing factory. The story of Apple and Chinese companies reflects a shift in power in the global technology industry.  As Apple products continue to be plagued with issues like discoloration, overheating, or rapid battery degradation, consumers are starting to question what they're paying for. Meanwhile, Chinese manufacturers seem to understand today's customer psychology. They want a high-performance device, beautiful camera, long-lasting battery and reasonable price instead of a shiny apple logo on the back.  If this trend continues, it is very likely that in just a few years, the concept of high-end phones will no longer be tied to Apple but will expand to Asian brands .  Just as Toyota beat out American automakers in the last century, China is following a similar path for the smartphone industry from being underestimated to imitating and finally leading.  Apple may still hold a position in the luxury segment, but that position is no longer absolute.  When iPhone 17s change color after just a few days of use, and Huawei or Xiaomi models offer superior performance at less than half the price, comparisons are inevitable.  And in a world of technology that changes by the hour, the difference between being ahead and falling behind is sometimes just a new generation of chips.  D

title: iPhone 17 Proを1ヶ月使った感想！メリット・デメリットはこれ…！
transcript: Hello, this is Yuni.  This time, I'm talking about the iPhone 17 Pro, and it's been a month since I bought my first iPhone in 10 years, so I thought I'd share my thoughts, the pros and cons, after using it for a month. First of all, I've been using Android for 10 years, and I recently switched from Android to an iPhone for the first time in 10 years. I started with an entry-level Android, and then a mid-range model, so I've now upgraded to a high-end model. I'd like to share with you the advantages and disadvantages I feel, so I hope this will be helpful for anyone considering switching to an iPhone .  Well then, let's get started . First, I'd like to talk about some of the benefits I've felt after using an iPhone for a month Well, first of all, the first one is, well, this iPhone 17.  This time, I got the Pro model , and I find the size of the Pro to be extremely easy to use .  The Android phone I've been using until now is , well, a model called Nash Phone 1, and in terms of size, it's pretty much the same size as a Pro MA.   If you compare them, well, it looks like this , this Android, the Android on the right , well, it's quite a bit larger, well, it's in terms of size .  When I actually bought the new phone, the size became smaller, so I was honestly worried that things like social media and videos that I'd been using on the large screen until now would become harder to see, but after using the iPhone 17 Pro for a month, I didn't feel any dissatisfaction at all with the smaller screen size .  In fact, I feel that the benefits of the smaller size are far greater.   What I particularly feel is that the smaller size makes it easier to operate.  Another thing is when you put it in Zumon's pocket.   There are some denim pockets that are quite shallow.  If you put something like that in your pocket, it will stick out a little .  When I squatted down with my phone in my pocket, it would dig into the base of my leg and cause quite a bit of pain, but with the size of the iPhone 17 Pro, I feel like it fits in the pocket much better.  If you're someone who normally uses a large screen , suddenly switching to the slightly smaller screen of the Pro, well, to be honest, I didn't feel that it was that much of a disadvantage. So, the second benefit is airdrop.  Anyway, this is convenient.  In particular, I use a MacBook as my main computer, and since I distribute videos all year round, I often exchange video materials and photographic materials between my smartphone and my computer.  At times like that, it was quite stressful that the connection between Android and Mac couldn't be made seamless, but now that AirDrop is available, data exchange can be made seamlessly , which I think is a huge benefit.  For people who don't usually exchange data between their PC and smartphone , I don't think it will be such a big benefit, but for people who exchange data for work and use a MacBook as their main device , I think it will be a really big benefit.  The third advantage is that the camera control buttons are very easy to use.   The camera control button is located at the bottom right of the display, and first of all, its location is great and it's extremely easy to use.  If you're holding a single-lens camera or other equipment in your left hand and you want to grab your smartphone, you can just take it out with one hand, start up the camera, and take a picture with the other hand , which is incredibly convenient .  Well, it's not impossible to do that kind of operation on Android, right?  You can press the power button twice to launch the camera, and press the volume button to take a picture, but the power button is on this side and the volume button is on this side, so you have to press them with your index finger and thumb, which means you have to hold the phone a little differently , and when you try to press the power button in the first place , your hand often covers the screen and you can't see the display.   If you use this as a camera control button, well , since it's located here, you can take pictures without your hand covering the display , so this is really, really the part that I find easy to use.  Especially this time, I switched from a mid-range Android to a high-spec, erm, iPhone, and the camera performance has improved so much that I've had a lot more opportunities to use the camera .  So the camera control button is really useful right now.  Well, that 's the fourth benefit.  Well, this is just my personal opinion, but I think it's fun that there are so many accessories, such as cases, made specifically for the iPhone.  Even when it comes to choosing a case, there are so many different types of iPhone cases out there. Of course, there are cases for Android models, but the one I used was a fairly minor model, so there aren't many dedicated cases available .  Well, if you look on Amazon there are quite a few varieties, but compared to the iPhone there are still far fewer.   It's not just cases, but all sorts of other things like photography equipment, there's even photography equipment for iPhones, but , well, most of that kind of smartphone equipment is generally made specifically for iPhones .  There are quite a few things that can't be used with Android, so you can also use photography equipment like that, and personally I think the wide variety of accessories available for the iPhone is really great.  The fifth and final benefit is that Apple Pay is just too convenient.  Well, I used Android.  This card didn't have FeliCa, so I couldn't use the transportation meter IC card, and I hardly used anything like Pay.  I used to only occasionally use Paypay or QR code payments, but recently I bought an iPhone and registered my transportation meter IC and credit card with Apple Pay , and I've been using it ever since. It's incredibly convenient.  Well, of course you can do this even if you don't have an iPhone, but this is the first time I've used it and I think it's incredibly convenient.  Anyway, now you do n't have to show your pass every time you get on the train .  Now, with just this smartphone, I can go through the ticket gates, and when I go shopping at convenience stores or supermarkets , I no longer have to take my credit card out of my wallet. Now I can pay for everything with my smartphone, which I find incredibly convenient .  After all, if you live in Japan and use it on a daily basis, you definitely need FeliCa at the very least, and it's inconvenient if it doesn't have it. This Android model didn't have FeliCa, so I felt that way a lot when I was using it, so I would really be happy if that were improved .  There are plenty of things you can do like this on Android as well, but personally, this is something I've never been able to do before, so I feel it's a huge benefit .  On the other hand, there are some aspects that I feel are disadvantages .  This is, well, the first one is , er, the back action.  Well, this is something I showed you in a previous video, but the back action on the iPhone is just too difficult to do.   To go back on Android, you swipe from the left side of the screen, and you can do the opposite as well .  You can also go back by swiping from right to left, but on an iPhone, you can only go back by swiping from left to right .  You can't go back by swiping from right to left, and the back action on the iPhone seems to vary quite a bit depending on the app.  There are some things you can go back to by swiping from left to right, but there are also some things you can't go back to unless you swipe up or down, and sometimes you can't go back at all unless you press the cross button in the top left, so it's quite a hassle .   With Android, you can basically just swipe from right to left or left to right to go back, so you can go back to almost everything, but with the iPhone, there are so many things you can't do or the way to go back is different that it's quite stressful.  This is something I noticed when I first bought the new one, but even now, a month later, I still ca n't get used to it.  When I use it, it can be a little frustrating if I can't go back .  And one more thing , which I feel is a disadvantage, is that the original keyboard app is extremely difficult to use.   I've heard that there are a lot of people who use iPhones and have third-party keyboard apps installed, so I guess I'll use a third-party app.  Yeah.  I guess the problem is that the original one is just too difficult to use .  Well, I knew from prior information that the original keyboard app was difficult to use, so I was thinking about installing a third-party one, but I thought I'd just try using the original one for now, so I've been using the original keyboard app for the past month, but it's just not possible.  What makes it difficult to use is that, well, you know , there are a lot of strange conversions that come up .  Instead of the literal conversion I created, it sometimes shows conversions of completely unrelated characters, so is there some kind of setting for this ? I have no idea, and the conversion just seems so strange. If there is a setting, I'd like to know about it , but first of all , the conversion is difficult to use, and also , when I'm typing on the keyboard and I want to move the cursor just one character to the right or one character to the left, there are no arrow keys.  For example, with Android, you're typing characters.  So, when you want to go back a little to a previous part, there are left and right arrow keys on this keyboard, and the cursor will move depending on the number of times you press them, so you can intuitively go back or forward .  So, you can easily erase or rewrite the text here, like in the middle part, but the original iPhone keyboard doesn't have arrow keys , so if you want to move the cursor back a little, you press and hold this blank button.  Well, with this, you can operate it like this, but it's really difficult to use .  If you have a cursor key, you can move just one character to the left and it will return to its original position with a single press, but with an iPhone, you have to press and hold the space and move one character to the right, which is quite difficult to adjust, and you have to press and hold the space every time, so if you want to move just one character, it's not easy to do it quickly.  I think this is the part that causes the most stress when using the original iPhone keyboard .  If you have any recommendations for the original keyboard, such as changing the settings to make it easier to use, or for third-party keyboards, I have no idea which ones are good, so I'd be grateful if you could let me know in the comments.  I really tried to use it for about a month and get used to it, but to be honest, I think I've reached my limit. yes.  So today, I'd like to share my impressions after switching from Android to iPhone and using it for a month , along with the pros and cons I've noticed .  Well, of course there are some disadvantages that I feel, but to be honest, overall I think that in my case there are far more advantages .  I didn't simply switch from an Android to an iPhone; in my case, I went from a mid-range to a high-end device, so the benefits of that are quite significant, but I also feel that there are benefits that come purely from the difference in OS .  Well, to be honest, it's only been a month, so I have n't mastered the iPhone at all, but even after using it for just a month, I can see that there are quite a few benefits .  If you are considering switching from Android to iPhone, I hope you will find this information useful.  So , thank you for watching the video until the end .  Well, we would like to continue to provide this kind of information in the future, so if you like it, please give it a thumbs up and subscribe to our channel See you in the next video.  bye bye. title: Redémarrage iPhone 17 Pro : méthode classique + astuce Siri 💡
transcript: Hi everyone, this is Easy Repair. Today I'm going to show you how to restart an iPhone 17 Pro or newer models with the latest version of iOS.  Don't forget to like the video, subscribe, and leave a comment if you need help.  Okay, let's go. There are several methods to restart your iPhone .  The first method involves using the volume up button and the side button simultaneously.  A menu will appear and you can swipe to turn it off.  But you will then have to manually press the side button to turn the iPhone back on. Another method is to use the power icon at the top of the screen.  Tap and slide to turn off.  Again, you will need to press the side button to turn it back on.  You can also go to settings, general, turn off [Music] and slide to turn off.  As with the other methods, you will need to restart the iPhone manually.  But a simpler method is to use Siri. To do this, go to settings.  Siri and Apple Smart, and make sure Siri is enabled.  Once Siri is activated, you can ask it to restart your iPhone.  For example, "Hey Siri, restart my iPhone."  Then confirm the order if necessary. Siri will automatically turn your iPhone off and on again , which is equivalent to a full reboot without touching the side button or connecting the iPhone to a charger.  There you go, your iPhone will restart completely.  I hope you found this video helpful.

It's said that the iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max will have the biggest innovation on the camera front, which is what now most distinguishes the non-Pro models from the Pro models, the introduction of variable aperture, something that many companies have already been working on in the past and that Apple has been following with interest and could finally arrive with the 2026 iPhone. The iPhone 18 Pro will therefore have this variable aperture that will allow for uniformity of results obtained during the day and at night not through software, but thanks to the management of the aperture in bright or slightly darker environments.  This innovation, in addition to improving the overall quality and uniformity of shots, will give users the opportunity to have a natural bokeh effect that can be customized without using software, but by acting directly on the aperture.  And it remains to be seen whether this technology will work, as already seen in the past on other smartphones, by choosing two different types of apertures, or whether Apple will provide the possibility, as on professional equipment, to have a range, a spectrum of apertures and to be able to move from a minimum limit to a maximum limit.  But let's get to the last piece of bad news now. Then there's another piece of news that could be either positive or negative. I'll let you decide. This one is about the foldable iPhone, the iPhone fold, or, as we said last week, the iPhone Ultra.  Well, it looks like Apple won't be able to stick to its plan to launch the new iPhone Ultra in early 2026, but this device could be postponed to 2027. The cover company had tried in every way to accelerate the development of its first foldable, but now it looks like the presentation will be skipped to 2027. There's also talk of a reduction in orders for the panels expected for this device, dropping from 13 million to 9 million, a sign that the company still expects to sell slightly fewer than it had initially estimated. Reportedly, the reason this device might be delayed is once again the hinge, an element that Apple has been struggling with for years to try to avoid all the usual compromises that most foldables have.  in particular the fact that after a certain number of openings the screen becomes marked in a rather irreparable way.  They will then try to hide the hinges entirely and offer an experience absolutely comparable to that of a regular iPhone, but this could still take a few months of development.  But let's get to this very last piece of news, which might not be so positive, but not so negative either.  I mean, we all know that the iPhone fold, the iPhone ultra, the foldable iPhone could have a really very high cost.  There had been speculations of €2000, €2002, € 2003, €2004, €2500 for this device, but today comes this indication, that is, that the iPhone fold could cost a little less than expected.  Of course, this doesn't mean it will cost €500 or so—it will still be an expensive device—but Apple has managed to significantly reduce the production cost of this device thanks to a revolution in the design of this very famous hinge. Clearly, we're talking about a ten- euro reduction, so there probably won't be a huge impact on the final cost of the foldable iPhone, but it's said that Apple is trying to do this in the coming months, perhaps by postponing the launch of the device a bit on all the elements that will compose it, so as to be able to offer this phone at a slightly more accessible price. However, considering that with the iPhone 17 Pro Max we are now well beyond the figures of 100, 16, 17 per device, already a minimum configured with a little more storage, in my opinion the price target of the foldable iPhone still remains around €2000, in the best case scenario.  It remains to be seen whether they will be able to refine something further or whether we should expect a device at these prices anyway.  But guys, that 's all for today's video.  Let me know what you think of all this news regarding the iPhone world.  And yes, as you saw, there was quite a bit of bad news and we, as always, will see you again soon in the next one.
